reasoning: Elon Musk has already won TIME's Person of the Year (2021), and it is exceptionally rare for non-presidents to win multiple times. While he garnered significant attention in early 2025 through his role in the DOGE initiative, he stepped back from this in May, and his business enterprises saw mixed results. More importantly, betting odds from mid-to-late 2025 strongly favor other candidates such as a newly elected Pope Leo XIV, Artificial Intelligence, and Donald Trump. Given his faded momentum in the latter half of the year and stiff competition, his probability is appropriately very low.
